probability.pow: Probability of Worthless (PoW) — the estimated probability that the option expires with no intrinsic value.
probability.weightedAverage: Weighted average across multiple probability estimation methods.
probability.bayesianCalibrated: Bayesian calibration adjusts raw model outputs using historical accuracy data to produce better-calibrated probabilities.
probability.originalBlackScholes: Standard Black-Scholes model probability using implied volatility from the option price.
probability.biasCorreected: Black-Scholes probability corrected for systematic overestimation bias observed in historical data.
probability.historicalIV: Probability estimated using historical realized volatility rather than implied volatility.

recoveryAdvantage.title: Recovery Advantage
recoveryAdvantage.description: Statistical advantage from probability recovery — options where multiple methods agree on high probability of expiring worthless.

margin.title: Estimated Margin
margin.description: Approximate margin requirement based on standard calculation. Actual margin may differ by broker.

annualizedReturn.title: Annualized Return
annualizedReturn.description: Premium received divided by margin requirement, annualized to a per-year basis.

supportLevel.title: Support Level
supportLevel.description: A price level where the stock has historically found buying interest, preventing further decline.

consecutiveBreaks.title: Consecutive Breaks
consecutiveBreaks.description: Number of consecutive times the stock price has broken through the support level in recent history.

howToRead.title: How to Read the Chart
howToRead.description: Each point represents predicted probability vs actual expiry rate for options in that probability bucket. Points closer to the diagonal line indicate better-calibrated predictions.
